edges.description: Paternal-allele GNAS alterations usually spare the renal PTH-resistance phenotype.
edges.target: Parent-of-origin sparing of renal PTH signaling

edges.description: Reduced GNAS signaling in skeletal and mesenchymal tissues drives AHO structural findings.
intermediate_mechanisms: altered Gs-alpha-cAMP signaling in skeletal and mesenchymal lineages
edges.target: AHO skeletal and soft-tissue developmental effects

edges.description: Disrupted endochondral bone growth shortens digits and metacarpals.
edges.source: AHO skeletal and soft-tissue developmental effects

edges.description: Skeletal growth impairment contributes to reduced stature.
edges.source: AHO skeletal and soft-tissue developmental effects

edges.description: Dysregulated mesenchymal ossification produces ectopic bone in soft tissue.
edges.source: AHO skeletal and soft-tissue developmental effects

nodes.description: Gs-alpha/cAMP pathway disruption in tissues relevant to skeletal growth and mesenchymal differentiation produces the Albright hereditary osteodystrophy pattern of short bones, short stature, and ectopic ossification.
nodes.id: AHO skeletal and soft-tissue developmental effects

evidence.snippet: The Gsα-cAMP cascade is pivotal for human skeletal growth, as evidenced by pathogenic mutations converging on this signaling pathway in a spectrum of skeletal dysplasias that overlap with AHO.
physical findings that variably include short bones, short stature, a stocky build, early-onset obesity and ectopic ossifications

nodes.description: Shortened digits and metacarpals are frequent AHO findings in PPHP.
Management is coordinated across pediatric/adult endocrinology, genetics, orthopedics, dentistry, nutrition, and neurodevelopmental services according to the patient's manifestations.

evidence.snippet: Overall, a coordinated and multidisciplinary approach from infancy through adulthood, including a transition programme, should help us to improve the care of patients affected by these disorders.

nodes.description: Ectopic ossification is a frequent AHO soft-tissue manifestation in PPHP.

Patients with PPHP and related GNAS disorders require baseline and follow-up screening for endocrine, skeletal, oral, metabolic, and ectopic-ossification complications even when classical PTH resistance is absent.

evidence.snippet: Patients should be screened at diagnosis and during follow-up for specific features, such as PTH resistance, TSH resistance, growth hormone deficiency, hypogonadism, skeletal deformities, oral health, weight gain, glucose intolerance or type 2 diabetes mellitus, and hypertension, as well as subcutaneous and/or deeper ectopic ossifications and neurocognitive impairment.
GNAS | GNAS complex locus | hgnc:4392 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (loss of function) in
PHP-Ia and PPHP are caused by heterozygous inactivating mutations in those exons of GNAS encoding the alpha subunit of the stimulatory guanine nucleotide-binding protein (Gsalpha)

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is reported in a subset of PPHP cases.

Orphanet records occasional obesity (29-5%) in PPHP. The 2026 openscientist hypothesis-search review (PMID:27991864, PMID:22511293) shows obesity in GNAS-associated disorders is driven by maternal Gsα loss in the dorsomedial hypothalamus (PHP1A-specific) and that PPHP patients (paternal mutations) are uniformly lean. Reports of obesity in PPHP cohorts likely reflect PHP1A misclassification or unrelated comorbidity and should be treated as an inconsistent finding pending clarified cohort data.

Because paternal GNAS contribution is normally suppressed in renal proximal tubule contexts where PTH acts, paternal coding variants classically do not produce the biochemical PTH-resistance triad of hypocalcemia, hyperphosphatemia, and elevated PTH.
nodes.id: Parent-of-origin sparing of renal PTH signaling

evidence.snippet: These features are also present in pseudopseudohypoparathyroidism (PPHP), but patients affected by this disorder do not show hormone resistance.

While Gsα is biallelically transcribed in most tissues, including bone and cartilage, the paternal Gsα allele is suppressed in a limited number of cells/tissues, including the proximal renal tubule, where PTH exerts critical actions.

nodes.description: Heterozygous inactivating variants in the GNAS exons encoding Gs-alpha cause PPHP when inherited or expressed from the paternal allele, producing an AHO phenotype rather than the maternal-allele PHP1A endocrine-resistance pattern.

biological_processes: adenylate cyclase-modulating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway

evidence.snippet: PHP-Ia and PPHP are caused by heterozygous inactivating mutations in those exons of GNAS encoding the alpha subunit of the stimulatory guanine nucleotide-binding protein (Gsalpha)
Therefore, Gsα mutations cause distinct clinical manifestations according to the affected parental allele.

nodes.description: Reduced linear growth is a frequent PPHP/AHO manifestation.
